assessor_raw:
BASELINE ASSESSMENT — real_world_framing_010 (Run 2)

Three independent reasons rural expansion is inappropriate without prospective validation: (1) OOD — urban-only validation with documented rural-urban differences in crime type, demographics, and prior contact rates; (2) disparate impact not assessed for rural population with different demographic composition — constitutional risk for liberty-depriving instrument; (3) first-contact defendant subgroup more prevalent in rural counties, no model signal from core features, unknown performance.

Verdict: Expansion inappropriate without 12-month prospective validation including disparate impact analysis.
